channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, on Sunday night, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Philadelphia defense, Tucker's misses help the Eagles to a 24-19 win in their showdown with Ravens
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Saquon Barkley vs. Derrick Henry was never supposed to be a head-to-head matchup. “It ain't basketball. It ain't like I go out there and tackle Derrick Henry,” Barkley said. “To be honest, I don't know if I really want to tackle Derrick Henry.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: ROSEMONT, Ill. (AP) — The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A landmark climate change case will open at the top UN court as island nations fear rising seas
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The top United Nations court will take up the largest case in its history on Monday, when it opens two weeks of hearings into what countries worldwide are legally required to do to combat climate change and help vulnerable nations fight its devastating impact.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn to observe World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: President Biden gives remarks for World AIDS Day
item.description: The White House is holding an event Sunday to commemorate World AIDS Day with survivors, their families, and advocates.
item.dc:creator: Mike Costanza, Fox 5

item.title: Heavy snow and numbing temperatures keep parts of the US in a deep freeze
item.description: Heavy snowfall and numbing temperatures kept parts of the U.S. in a deep freeze Sunday as the Thanksgiving holiday weekend draws to a close.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Southwest Airlines to end cabin service earlier on flights: Here's why
item.description: You'll be asked to fasten your seatbelts and return your seat to the upright position earlier than before.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump selects veteran law enforcement officer Chad Chronister for DEA administrator
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump announced he would nominate Florida Sheriff Chad Chronister to serve as the administrator for the Drug Enforcement Agency. “I am pleased to nominate Sheriff Chad Chronister for Administrator of the Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA),” he wrote in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he'll nominate Kash Patel as FBI director to remake the agency. Here's what happens next
item.description: Donald Trump’s plan to nominate Kash Patel as FBI director sets the stage for a fresh round of turbulence at a law enforcement agency tasked with protecting the homeland and investigating federal crimes.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about Kash Patel, Trump’s pick to lead the FBI
item.description: Kash Patel has called for radical changes at the FBI and was a fierce and vocal critic of the bureau’s work as it investigated ties between Russia and Donald Trump’s 2016 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump taps Kash Patel for FBI director, an ally who would aid in his effort to upend law enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump says he will nominate Kash Patel to serve as FBI director, turning to a fierce ally to upend America’s premier law enforcement agency and rid the government of perceived “conspirators.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida grandfather, 71, fatally shot by neighbor during noise dispute; suspected gunman claims self-defense
item.description: A 71-year-old Florida man was allegedly shot to death by his downstairs neighbor in Lauderdale Lakes after the neighbor complained about loud music.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Credit card debt is soaring – what to know about getting out of it
item.description: With credit card debt reaching an all-time high, now is the time for the most pertinent questions and answers surrounding a difficult reality many Americans are facing.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5

item.title: Bob Bryar, former My Chemical Romance drummer, dies at 44
item.description: (WGHP) — Bob Bryar, the drummer of the rock band My Chemical Romance from 2004 to 2014 has died, TMZ reports. He was 44. According to TMZ, Bryar was found dead in his Tennessee home Tuesday after last being seen alive on November 4.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz saga moves toward final act in House
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz’s (R-Fla.) time in Congress may be over, but the saga over the House Ethics Committee’s report on its years-long investigation into him is in its final act.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KRON4

item.title: Pennsylvania restaurant ordered to pay $184,000 for withholding tips
item.description: PITTSBURGH, Pa. (WHTM) - A Pennsylvania restaurant was ordered to pay $184,000 in back wages, damages, and penalties after an investigation found they withheld tips. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Earl Holliman, 'The Rainmaker' and 'Twilight Zone' actor, dead at 96
item.description: Award-winning actor Earl Holliman has died at the age of 96, according to multiple reports. Holliman died Monday in hospice care at his home in Studio City according to his spouse, Craig Curtis, who told The Hollywood Reporter.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KTLA 5

item.title: South Carolina mayor dies in crash while being ‘pursued’ by deputy, just days after his police force quit
item.description: McColl Mayor George Garner II died in a vehicle crash in Mechanicsville just days after his town's entire police force resigned, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Don't fall for this secret Santa gift scam
item.description: A secret Santa-inspired scam is making its rounds again on social media, the Better Business Bureau warns. You may see it being called "Secret Sister" or "Secret Santa Dog" – but either way, it's illegal.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Chicago-area police officer killed while responding to 'armed offender' at bank, suspect charged
item.description: Oak Park Police Detective Allan Reddins died Friday after being shot while responding to a call about an "armed offender" seen leaving a Chase Bank, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why your favorite catalogs are smaller this holiday season
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Honey, they shrunk the catalogs. While retailers hope to go big this holiday season, customers may notice that the printed gift guides arriving in their mailboxes are smaller. Many of the millions of catalogs getting sent to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WGN 9

item.title: Kentucky boy in coma after jumping from truck as intoxicated father drove to buy beer
item.description: A Kentucky man is facing charges after he went out for beer run while intoxicated and his son ended up in a coma after jumping out of the truck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the plastic pollution crisis as treaty talks conclude in South Korea
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — The world’s nations will wrap up negotiating a treaty this weekend to address the global plastic pollution crisis.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Washington boy, 12, steals grandfather's car, drives 160 miles before he is stopped
item.description: A 12-year-old Washington state boy was arrested and taken into custody after he allegedly stole his grandfather's car and led deputies on a short pursuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lake Effect Snow Warning extended; Sunday closures and travel alerts
item.description: (WJET/WFXP) — After a break in the snow Saturday, snow-covered roads and cars are still a major issue for thousands across the region after more than two feet fell, and some areas even saw more than three feet as crews continue to work around the clock to clear the roads, with interstate restriction
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: Woman accused of knife attack on family at Thanksgiving
item.description: UPDATE: The suspect, 55-year-old Lachunda Johnson has been charged with Aggravated Assault/DV, Aggravated Assault, and Domestic Assault, according to Memphis Police. MPD says Johnson is the mother of one of the stabbing victims. *** MEMPHIS, Tenn.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: SF burglary victim’s medical device returned after GPS tracker leads to suspect
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- A victim of a vehicle burglary in San Francisco earlier this month had her luggage and medical equipment returned after a GPS tracker led police to the suspect, according to the San Francisco Police Department. On Nov.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: Colorado officer uses kid's bike during pursuit
item.description: A Lone Tree police officer went above and beyond to try and catch a theft suspect who fled on a scooter in a neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KRON4

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (WIAT) — Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game. Burnett Jr., a linebacker who was injured during the Magic City Classic on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 tips to avoid overspending this holiday season
item.description: Nearly 30% of credit card users are still paying off last year's holiday debt. Here are five tips to avoid overspending.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Venezuelan gang members linked to violent apartment takeover arrested in New York City
item.description: Tren de Aragua members Denyeer Aramillo Meneses, 23, and Edison Pena Angulo, 25, were arrested in the Bronx. Both were pictured in a viral video of a Colorado armed break in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hot showers can harm skin, hair health, dermatologists say
item.description: Scalding showers can lead to the removal of skin layers and essential oils from your body, which can be damaging.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KRON4

item.title: Record Black Friday, holiday shopping expected despite inflation
item.description: A Black Friday shopping frenzy is expected once again this year despite financial constraints facing many across America.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Disney agrees to $43 million settlement in gender pay lawsuit
item.description: The agreement settles allegations initially made in a 2019 lawsuit filed by Disney employee LaRonda Rasmussen, who claimed she was getting paid less than her male counterparts in the same role.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9
